Something large moving along the ridge after dark
Fresh tracks where there shouldn't be any
A shape watching from the tree line at dusk
Claw marks gouged into the bark about eight feet up
A dead animal left half-eaten by the watering hole
Something with too many legs skittering across the old stones
A low growl from underneath the footbridge, but nothing visible
Drag marks leading from the livestock pen into the tall grass
Two sets of eyes reflecting the firelight just past the clearing
A hunter came back white as chalk and wouldn't say what he saw
Birds circling over the hillside for hours, never landing
Something tall standing perfectly still between the trees
Prints in the mud the size of a dinner plate
Fur caught on the fence posts, dark and coarse like nothing local
The dogs refused to cross the stream, kept whimpering and pulling back
A sound like breathing coming from inside the collapsed mine
The shepherd's boy swears he saw something walk upright through the meadow
Deep scratches on the outside of the storage shed door
Whatever it was, it moved too fast for its size
A trail of broken branches leading up into the hills, shoulder height
Something splashing in the pond well after midnight
Livestock found huddled in the corner of the pen, trembling
An outline against the sky on the ridge, gone when the clouds shifted
Three deer carcasses piled together near the old foundation
A woman gathering herbs said something followed her along the tree line
Feathers everywhere by the coop, but no blood and no bodies
The watchman heard hooves on stone, but the road was empty
A burrow dug under the wall, wide enough for a person to crawl through
Something howled from the direction of the ruins, long and deliberate
The fish in the lower creek have all gone, like they fled downstream
A shape moving through the fog, low to the ground and quick
Tooth marks on the well rope, clean through the fibers
Whatever killed those rabbits didn't eat them, just left them arranged in a line
A farmhand found a den in the hillside, freshly dug and lined with bones
The horses won't go near the north trail anymore
Muddy handprints on the outside of the shutters, but the fingers were wrong
A trader said he saw something crouched on the old bridge at dawn
An awful stench rolling down from the caves when the wind shifts
The goats got loose and all ran the same direction, toward the village center
Scratch marks on the inside of the abandoned watchtower door
A woodcutter found tufts of grey hair snagged on a low branch, thick as rope
Something dug up the root cellar from the outside, clean through the packed earth
The patrol found a kill site near the stream: bones cracked open for marrow
A child said she saw something with a long tail slip under the granary at dusk
The cats have been gathering on rooftops at night, all staring the same direction
Hoof prints in soft ground, but deeper than any horse should press
A fence rail snapped clean, not rotted: something pushed through it
The beekeeper says her hives went quiet for three days, then started again as if nothing happened
A trader's pack mule refused to take the valley path and nearly threw its load
Something left a track in the frost that melted by morning, but the shape was wrong
Scales found on the bank of the upper pond, iridescent and palm-sized
A hunter's snare was torn apart from inside, and whatever was caught left no blood
The night watch heard clicking from the rooftops, like claws on clay tile
Livestock were found with bite marks that didn't break the skin, just deep bruises
The old well smells different now: something musky and animal
A farmer found a clutch of eggs buried in his compost heap, leathery and warm
Something stripped the bark off three trees in a row, smooth as a knife cut
The chickens stopped laying the same week the howling started
A guard on the wall saw something cross the road on all fours, fast and low
Webbing found strung between the rafters of the abandoned barn, thick as cord
The shepherd lost two animals in one night: no remains, no tracks, just gone
Something left a pile of regurgitated bones near the watering trough
A fisherwoman pulled up a net full of dead minnows, every one of them bitten in half
The ground near the old foundation is torn up like something was digging in a frenzy
A courier said his horse shied at the crossroads and he couldn't get it past the marker stone
There's a depression in the tall grass the size of a sleeping ox, pressed flat and still warm
Claw marks running down the outside of the chimney, from roof to ground
A trapper found one of his steel traps bent open and the bait gone
The owls have gone silent for a week, which the old-timers say means something bigger moved in
Whatever crossed the field left a trail of flattened wheat in a straight line
A woodsman found a tree pushed over at the base, not cut: pushed
The millpond water turned murky overnight and the fish are floating belly-up
A sentry reported hearing something large breathing on the other side of the palisade
The goatherd says one of his animals came back with a tuft of something's fur in its horns
Bones arranged in a small pile outside the gate, picked perfectly clean
A runner coming in from the outpost said the trail smelled like a dead thing for half a mile
Something heavy has been crossing the footbridge at night: the planks are cracking
The dogs howled in unison at midnight and then went completely silent
A pile of fish heads left on the dock, neatly stacked: no one here did that
A guard found a handprint on the inner wall, too high to reach without climbing
The well bucket came up with scratch marks inside it, deep and parallel
Feathers from a bird of prey found scattered near the granary, but no body
A tanner reported something watching him from the drying racks at night
The orchard has claw gouges on the lower trunks, fresh sap still running
Whatever's been at the grain stores leaves tracks that vanish on hard ground
A patrol found a section of fence torn out and carried twenty paces into the field
The ducks on the pond won't leave the center of the water anymore
Something left a deep furrow in the mud beside the road, like it was dragged
A merchant swears his donkey won't pass the old milestone since last week
The forester found a deer hung in a tree fork, ten feet off the ground
Paw prints in ash by the firepit, five-toed and wider than a man's hand
A low vibration in the ground near the eastern boundary, like something tunneling
The night patrol heard wings overhead, heavy and slow, but saw nothing
A child found a shed antler near the wall that's twice the size of any local deer
Something tore the leather cover off the rain barrel and drank it dry
The charcoal burner says something has been scattering his stacks in the night
Slime trails on the cobblestones near the well, wide as a belt
A shepherd's crook was found snapped in two out in the field, no one around
The stable horses kicked their stalls to splinters during the night, all at once
A hunter says he found a game trail where every hoofprint pointed the same direction: away
Whatever left those marks has claws that curve inward, not outward
A fisherfolk reported something bumping the underside of his boat on the lake
The guard dogs won't eat and they keep staring at the tree line
A woodcutter heard something mimicking birdsong, but wrong: too slow, too deep
The roof thatch on the outermost house was torn open from above
A woman drawing water at dawn saw something slip beneath the surface of the well
Tracks in the garden soil that start in the middle: no approach, no departure, just a circle of prints
Something peeled the bark off the gateposts like stripping hide
The herb gatherer found crushed plants in a path too wide for any person
A sentry smelled something rank and sweet on the wind, worse than any carrion
A farmer's scarecrow was torn down and the pole was bitten through
The reed beds along the river have been trampled flat in one direction
Something scratched a groove around the base of the signal post, all the way around
The miller says something has been drinking from the millrace at night: he can hear the lapping
A patrol found a cave entrance that wasn't there last season, freshly clawed open
Broken eggshells near the hilltop, leathery and dark: not from any bird
A hunter's blind was demolished overnight, the supports snapped like kindling
Something large crossed the footpath and left a smell that lingered for hours
The blacksmith's dog crawled under the forge and wouldn't come out for two days
Hairs snagged on the bridge railing, coarse and reddish and longer than a hand span
A fisherman on the lake saw a wake with no boat and no wind behind it
The lookout spotted movement on the far hill: too big for a person, too upright for an animal
A section of stone wall was knocked inward, and the stones weigh more than a man can lift
The mushroom gatherer found a patch of forest floor scraped to bare earth in a perfect circle
Something has been gnawing the corner posts of the livestock shelter
A night watchman heard chewing sounds from the alley and found nothing but wet marks on the cobbles
A trader's horse arrived at the gate alone, lathered and wild-eyed, reins snapped
The old hollow tree by the road has fresh scratch marks inside, like something bedded down
A scout found a watering hole muddied and trampled by something with three-toed feet
The watchtower lantern was knocked from its hook: something struck the tower hard enough to shake it
Tracks along the ridgeline, spaced too far apart for anything walking
A child heard purring from the storm drain, deep and rattling like a grinding stone
The smokehouse door was pried open and the hanging meat was taken, hooks and all
A courier says his lantern attracted something on the road that paced him for a mile
The vineyard stakes were used as scratching posts: deep gouges, fresh splinters
A farmer found his irrigation ditch dammed with stones and packed mud, but no beaver sign
The pigeons won't roost on the east side of the square anymore
Something left a trail of broken pottery outside the storehouse, like it knocked things over in the dark
A guard heard a splash in the cistern and found the water fouled with a musky stink
The hay in the loft was scattered and pressed flat, as though something large slept there
A traveling herbalist found claw marks on a signpost, fresh enough to still smell of sap
An apprentice on an errand saw something duck behind a wall that was too tall to hide behind
The woodpile was knocked over and rearranged: not scattered, rearranged
A trapper found his deadfall sprung and the bait stone flipped over, but nothing caught
The frogs stopped singing at the pond, all at once, then started again an hour later
A guard on patrol smelled wet fur and heard something heavy shift its weight nearby
The root cellar hatch has new gouges around the latch, like something tried to work it open
A farmwife found tracks on her doorstep at dawn: clawed, heavy, and headed toward the house
Something disturbed the cairn on the hill: stones moved aside and the earth beneath dug up
The lookout swears he saw two shapes moving together through the moonlit field, loping in tandem
A scout returned early and said the tree canopy in the eastern wood was shaking with no wind
The water trough was overturned and the stone base cracked: something strong did that
Fur and blood on the top rail of the fence, too high for a fox, wrong color for a bear
A woodsman heard heavy footfalls keeping pace with him on the other side of the ridge
The market cats are jumpy: arched backs and low hissing whenever the wind shifts east
A patrol found feathers from something huge: one quill as long as an arm
The gravel pit shows signs of something large rolling or thrashing in it
A farm dog came home with a gash on its flank, deep and clean, from something sharp
The rain gutters on the outermost buildings were torn loose, bent down like something climbed them
A forester found sap smeared across tree trunks at head height, in a line stretching fifty paces
The night soil collector says something was moving through the back alleys on all fours, low and fast
A guard heard heavy breathing through the wall and found five-clawed scrapes on the mortar next morning
The clothesline was pulled down, and the torn fabric had tooth marks: small, sharp, many rows
A farmer found a stone wall section warm to the touch at dawn, like something had leaned against it all night
The creek bed has new prints in it, webbed and deep, heading upstream
An herbalist noticed all the foxglove in the east meadow was trampled flat by something heavy
The shutters on the outermost cabin were clawed from outside: long, deep grooves in the wood
A boy gathering firewood heard a sound like stones grinding together deep in the underbrush
The bats in the old barn have gone: all of them, overnight
Something clipped the top of the hedgerow clean off, as though bitten through at shoulder height
A scout found a patch of ground near the hilltop where the soil was turned over and steaming
The dogs have been barking at the same spot on the wall for three nights running
A woman on the evening walk heard something cracking bones behind the woodshed
The footpath through the meadow has a new smell to it: sharp, like a den
A nighttime sentry saw something climb the outer wall and drop back down when he raised his lantern
The apiary was overturned and the frames were chewed, not clawed: chewed
A carter found his wheel ruts from the previous day crossed by something with a stride longer than his cart
The geese were frantic at dawn, honking and beating their wings against the pen
Something pulled a fence post out of the ground and left it leaning against the next one
A forager found a game trail where every branch at head height was snapped back, freshly broken
The sentry tower smells like wet animal, but there's nothing inside
A child drawing water said something moved under the well cover and she heard it hiss
The cobblestones near the east gate have parallel scratch marks, like something dragged its claws while walking
A farmer's field has a trench through it, straight and deep, like something burrowed just below the surface
The grain sacks in the storehouse were torn open from the top, not gnawed: torn
A traveling tinker said he found a campsite on the road completely wrecked, but no bodies
The hanging lanterns on the outer path were knocked down, chain and all
A patrolman noticed the moss on the north wall was scraped off at a height of about nine feet
Something left a deposit of pellets near the old well: hair and bone fragments, tightly packed
The ferry rope across the river was frayed halfway through, and the fraying had tooth marks
A shepherd heard a scream from the treeline that started low and rose to a pitch no animal should make
The log pile behind the workshop was scattered, and several logs had deep punctures in them
A tracker found prints near the settlement that showed something circled the perimeter twice before leaving
The copper gutters on the meeting hall roof were bent and one section was torn away entirely
A woman heard tapping on her wall at night, rhythmic and deliberate, then silence
The compost heap behind the kitchens was dug through and the bones in it were gone
A patrol found a tree with its roots exposed, not fallen: something tried to pull it out of the ground
Claw marks on the inside of the old kiln, as though something was trapped and dug its way out
The water level in the cistern drops every night by the same amount, but there's no leak
A carter's horse balked at the gate and would not enter the settlement until blindfolded
The tar pots by the wall were tipped over and something tracked through them, leaving dark prints heading east
A woodcutter found his axe pulled from the stump and thrown several paces into the brush
The night watch reported a sound like something dragging itself along the outer wall
A field hand saw something large leap the boundary ditch in a single bound and vanish into the wheat
The stones around the campfire ring were rearranged, and the ashes were scattered as though something sat in them
A patrol found a strand of something fibrous and strong wrapped around a fence post, like a snare
The leather on the hitching post was chewed through and the brass ring was scored with tooth marks
A sentry heard a single, deliberate thump against the gate at the darkest hour, then nothing
The straw in the outer stable was matted down in a shape too large for any of the horses
A gatherer found broken shells near the riverbank: thick, ridged, and bigger than a fist
Something visited the fish drying racks in the night: every third fish was taken, evenly spaced
The herb garden has a track through it where everything was crushed flat in a single pass
A boy tending the fire saw something reflected in the window that wasn't in the room
The tannery hides strung on the line were pulled down and one was carried off: the largest one
A patrolman found matted hair clinging to the underside of the bridge, fresh and rank
The warning bell rope was found gnawed and frayed at a height no rodent could reach
A woman near the river heard gurgling in the reeds and saw the stalks parting in a line moving upstream
The stone step at the east gate is cracked: something heavy landed on it from above
A merchant arriving at dawn said he saw shapes in the mist that moved against the wind
The rain barrel lid was punctured from inside and the water smelled of iron
A lumberjack heard something breathing in the sawpit and found long scratches in the crossbeam
The stable cat brought something to the doorstep that no one could identify: part of a limb, scaled
A sentry reported that the treeline flickered with reflected eyes when he swept his lantern across it
Turf near the outer road was peeled back in strips, like something rooting with great force
A farmer says his dog dug up a jawbone in the field that didn't belong to anything he recognized
The chimney of the outermost house was plugged with packed earth and sticks: from above
A guard heard what sounded like laughter from the old ruin, thin and high-pitched
The marker stones along the perimeter trail were knocked over, every other one
A woman found deep impressions in her garden bed shaped like a body curled on its side
The stockade wall has a section where the timbers are bowed inward, as though something leaned hard
A traveler arrived shaken, saying something kept pace with his torch on the road for over an hour
The woodpecker holes in the dead elm are stuffed with something fibrous and foul-smelling
A sentinel heard claws on the gate latch, working it: not scratching, working it
The outhouse behind the barracks was pushed off its base, and the pit underneath was disturbed
A child saw something coiled in the branches of the old oak, longer than the branch itself
The stone trough at the crossroads has new tooth marks on its rim: wide-set and deep
A forester found saplings broken at exactly the same height in a line stretching through the wood
The hatch to the storm cellar was opened from outside and left ajar, with mud tracked down the steps
A guard says something pressed against the gate hard enough to leave an imprint in the wood grain
The ropes securing the hay bales in the loft were snapped, and the bales were shoved aside
A woman heard scraping on her roof tiles at midnight and found three tiles displaced by morning
The old signpost at the fork was uprooted and driven back into the ground at an angle
A hunter found blood on the rocks by the stream but no carcass and no trail leading away
The fence around the livestock pen has a gap bent outward, the wire twisted like someone wrung it
A night fisherman on the bank heard heavy footsteps in the shallows behind him and packed up without looking
The mortar between the stones in the perimeter wall is scratched out at one spot, methodically
A laborer found claw marks on the underside of the bridge timbers, as though something hung there
The barrel of salted fish was dragged from the storehouse and found fifty paces away, empty
A scout reports that the bird calls in the eastern wood stopped at a sharp boundary, like a line drawn in the forest
The lead goat came back with scratches across its shoulders and a notch bitten out of one ear
A sentry saw something on the wall walk that dropped over the far side before he could reach it
The grain chute in the mill was jammed with something wedged inside: a knot of matted fur and teeth
A rider says his horse threw him when they passed a particular stretch of hedge and nothing would calm it
The undergrowth near the old well has been flattened in a wide circle, pressed outward from the center
A guard found a dead snake by the gate, bitten cleanly in half by something with a very wide jaw
The lumber stack has deep furrows raked across the end grain, and sawdust was scattered in a wide arc
A woman found a handprint on her flour-dusted windowsill that had too many fingers
The dovecote was raided in the night and the door wasn't opened: something reached through the slats
A child heard humming from the dry well, low and continuous, and the stones around the rim were warm
The watchfire went out twice in one night, and each time the ashes were scattered outward
A tracker says whatever made those prints was walking on its toes, not its whole foot
The leather hinges on the smokehouse door were bitten through: not cut, bitten
A farmhand saw the grain in the field ripple in a line, like something moved beneath it
The lookout noticed a shadow on the wall that didn't match anything casting it
A guard found a rock the size of a man's head wedged into the gate frame, thrown from outside
The potter's kiln had claw marks scored into the clay dome, still warm from the night's firing
A woman gathering water at the well heard a second bucket drop when she hadn't let go of hers
Something peeled the tin patch off the storehouse roof and left the edges curled inward
A hunter lost his catch from a hanging line: the rope was cut at a height he couldn't reach
The standing stones outside the settlement have fresh scrape marks, white against the grey
A night sentry heard wingbeats so heavy they stirred the dust on the battlement
The footpath to the spring has new prints pressed into solid clay, deeper than any boot
A courier says the mile markers on the eastern road have been clawed, and each one more deeply than the last
The grain threshing floor has long gouges across it, like something dragged heavy claws in passing
A shepherd found wool snagged on a rock face at a height no sheep could reach
The well chain was pulled taut and bent at the crossbar, as if something tried to climb down on it
A woodsman says the moss on the northern rocks has been scraped clean in patches shaped like shoulders
The garden wall has a gap where three stones were pushed through from outside, not pulled out
A sentry heard a wet slapping sound against the palisade, repeated slowly like something testing the wood
The old oak's lowest branch was torn off and dragged into the underbrush
A trapper's skinning knife was found driven into a tree at chest height, blade first
The watchtower stairs had something sticky on the handrail: clear, viscous, and it smelled faintly of fish
A patrolman found the mud at the river ford churned up by something heavy moving in circles
The chicken run fence was peeled back from the bottom like someone rolling up a carpet
A farm boy saw something perched on the granary roof at twilight that was bigger than any bird he knew
The bark on the old cedar has been rubbed smooth on one side, up to a height of ten feet
A woman heard something chewing on the corner of her house in the small hours: deliberate, rhythmic
The cobblestones near the south gate have a greasy film on them that wasn't there yesterday
A guard found three fence posts pulled free and laid across the path like a barrier
The millstream has a new odor: heavy and animal, strongest at dawn
A hunter's cached provisions were found torn open, and the cache stones were scattered ten paces out
The latrine pit behind the barracks was disturbed: something dug partway in from the side
An herbalist found tracks in her garden that show something walking on two legs but landing toe-first
The old cellar grate was bent upward from below, the iron bars spread apart
A trader found shed skin caught on the bushes near the road, translucent and the length of his forearm
The sawyer's log cradle was overturned and the chain securing it was pulled straight through the ring bolt
A sentry heard something panting on the far side of the gate, low and fast, then heard it leave at a run
The plaster on the inner wall of the storehouse has five parallel grooves raked through it
A boy watching the sheep at dusk saw something move under the water of the stock pond without surfacing
The fence wire along the east field was stretched so tight it hummed, pulled by something passing through
A night watchman found a half-eaten turnip on the wall walk, bitten by something with a mouth wider than a man's
The pottery shards behind the kiln were ground to powder in one spot, as if something heavy stood there and shifted its weight
A courier arrived and said his lantern glass was cracked by something that struck it in flight on the road
The stone drinking trough has a chip out of the rim that matches the shape of a bite
A forester says the animal trails in the eastern wood have all shifted south by a quarter mile
The timber stack behind the carpentry had bark stripped from the topmost logs, fifteen feet up
A child sleeping near the window woke to find condensation on the glass in the shape of a pressed face
The cistern cover was slid aside during the night: it takes two men to move it
A guard noticed a patch of frost on the wall that didn't match the weather, shaped roughly like a splayed hand
The pig trough was flipped over and the iron feet were bent flat against the base
A scout says the game has moved out of the eastern wood entirely: not thinned, emptied
The drying nets on the rack were shredded in strips too even to be wind damage
A woman found a row of small stones lined up on her windowsill that she didn't place there
The bellows in the smithy were found punctured by something that punched clean through the leather
A farmhand heard hooting from the empty barn at midnight: not an owl, too deep and too slow
The soil near the boundary marker is packed hard in a circle, as though something sat there for hours
A man returning from the fields at dusk saw something tall step behind the barn and not reappear
The grain silo hatch was scored with parallel marks, the wood splintered outward
A trapper's net was found wadded into a ball and shoved under a rock, as though hidden by something with hands
The old signpost near the bridge leans toward the wood now: the base is scored as if something used it as a scratching post
A fisher saw something long and dark rise and sink in the deepest part of the lake, without breaking the surface fully
The bark mulch around the orchard trees has been kicked up in a line, like something sprinted through at speed
A guard says he felt something brush past him in the dark at the wall base, but his lantern showed nothing
The tar bucket by the gate was punctured from the side and the contents leaked across the path in dark streaks
A woman collecting eggs at dawn found something had nested in the back of the henhouse: matted fiber and a foul smell, but nothing present
The goat pen rails show teeth marks at an angle too steep for any goat to make
A night patrol found drag marks leading into the culvert under the road, fresh and deep
The hay rake left standing in the field was snapped in two and the halves were found twenty paces apart
A child heard something scratching inside the walls of the house, moving from one end to the other slowly
The lookout says the grass on the hillside parts in a line at night, as if something walks through it that he can't see
A farmer's water jug was found crushed at the field edge, and the water had soaked into prints too large for a man
The stonework on the well was warm on one side yesterday morning, as if something had pressed against it
A carter's whip was taken from his bench overnight and found wrapped around a post near the gate
The frost on the field showed a path of melted prints leading from the wood to the settlement and back again
A sentinel watching the treeline at dusk saw something raise itself on hind legs to look over the brush, then drop
The fish weir in the stream was dismantled: the stakes pulled out and arranged on the bank
A laborer heard clicking and chattering from the old cellar, like something communicating in a language of sounds
The tin sheeting on the lean-to was peeled back and the edges show puncture marks from gripping claws
A patrol found a boulder moved to block a trail that was clear the day before: the drag mark in the dirt was straight
The grain cart left in the yard overnight had the tailgate pried off and the latch was bitten through
A woman says she saw something slide off the roof of the storehouse at dawn, hit the ground on all fours, and run
The tallow candles in the outer guardhouse melted unevenly, bowed toward the window as if drawn by heat from outside
A guard says his relief found him staring at the treeline and he couldn't remember the last hour of his watch
The ferry cable has new grooves worn into it at one point, as though something hung from it and swung
A scout found a series of holes dug along the road, evenly spaced, each about a forearm deep
The leather apron hung on the workshop door was pulled down and the neck strap was chewed through
A shepherd's dog returned from the hills with quills embedded in its muzzle, from something not local
The wooden shutters on the lower floor of the storehouse have impressions pressed into them from outside, like something leaned its weight there
A farmer found the scarecrow relocated to a different part of the field, standing upright and intact
The water barrel behind the tavern has a circular hole in the side at ground level, gnawed clean through
A courier says he saw something standing in the road ahead of him at night that dropped to all fours and moved aside
The dry leaves under the old oak are pressed into a perfect bowl shape, as though something bedded down and circled before resting
A woman found parallel scratches on her front door at knee height, deep enough to show bare wood
The stone base of the well has a crack radiating from a single point of impact, like something struck it with tremendous force
A sentinel's torch was snuffed by something that passed overhead and he felt the wind of it
The wattle fence between the gardens was pushed flat in one spot, the woven branches still intact but pressed to the ground
A tracker found the remains of a campfire that wasn't made by any person: the stones were wrong, the arrangement was wrong
The lead horse in the stable had bite marks on its mane, hair torn out in clumps
A boy tending the forge fire heard tapping on the chimney cap, regular and patient
The catfish in the lower pond have been surfacing more than usual, and two had gashes along their sides
A patrol noticed that the boundary cairns had been visited: stones disturbed, lichen scraped, the ground around them trampled
The door of the meat locker was found ajar and the bolt was bent sideways, not broken: bent
A fisherman says the herons have abandoned the north shore and he hasn't seen one in a week
The stone wall near the grazing land had a section rebuilt during the night: clumsily, with the wrong stones, but rebuilt
A guard found slick wet marks on the wall walk at dawn, evaporating in the sun before he could show anyone
The privy behind the meeting hall was knocked off its base and dragged a short distance toward the road
A farmhand noticed that every post in the western fence line had claw marks at the same height, exactly
The old copper weathervane was torn from its mount and found on the ground, punctured by something's grip
A woman washing clothes at the stream heard heavy breathing from the reeds and left her basket behind
The packed earth of the threshing floor had new impressions in it: something lay there flat and heavy
A sentry heard what sounded like digging under the foundation of the palisade, steady and purposeful
The last of the autumn fruit was stripped from the orchard trees overnight, but the fallen fruit on the ground was untouched
A trapper says his scent markers along the boundary trail have been obliterated: scraped, urinated on, and covered over
The goose that serves as the settlement alarm has been silent for two days and won't leave its shelter
A woodcutter's saw was found jammed between two trees with the teeth bent backward, as if something tried to pull it through
The mud near the old foundation shows a set of prints where something stood in one spot for a long time, shifting its weight
A guard on the east wall heard a long, drawn-out exhale from directly below and looked down to see nothing
The storage pit was opened from outside and the covering logs were stacked beside it, carefully
A woman returning home at dusk saw something in the garden that she first mistook for a stump, until it moved
The dogs in the settlement have been sleeping in a tight group rather than spread out: a pack behavior they don't usually show
A scout reports that the undergrowth at the forest edge is bent inward, as if something large enters the wood at the same point every night
The stone lintel over the back gate has a new hairline crack, and fragments of claw were found embedded in the mortar
A farmer noticed his scarecrow's shadow was joined by a second one at sunset, but when he looked there was only the scarecrow
The copper kettle left hanging at the camp was dented by something that gripped it and squeezed
A woman found feathers in her chimney: too large for any bird that could fit inside, and they smelled of musk
The patrol says the usual fox tracks near the settlement have been replaced by something heavier using the same trails
A man checking his traps heard two distinct voices calling to each other in the wood: neither was human, but they were communicating
The gate bar was lifted during the night and set carefully on the ground beside the gateposts
A scout found a tree with a hole bored into the trunk at chest height, clean and round, packed with something dark
The old cart path through the field has new ruts in it that are too narrow for wheels and too deep for hooves
A night sentry saw the grass of the outer field flatten in a wave moving toward the settlement, then stop at the wall
The salt lick set out for the livestock was shattered overnight, and the fragments were scattered in a pattern
